original: Oumuamua first detected by scientists, 400 meters long and reddish, first known interstellar object in our solar system, thought to be of possible alien origin (since thought to be slice of small planet)
McCaughey septuplets (all 7 of them!) born to Bobbi and Kenny McCaughey in Des Moines, Iowa. First set of septuplets to survive infancy.
Apollo 12's Charles Conrad and Alan Bean become the 3rd and 4th humans on the Moon
Innovative video game console the Nintendo Wii is released in North America by Nintendo PresidentSatoru Iwata
"Harry Potterand the Deathly Hallows – Part 1" the 7th film based on the books byJ. K. Rowlingis released worldwide
Pop duo Milli Vanilli are stripped of their Grammy Award after it is learned they did not sing on their award-winning "Girl You Know It's True" album
Comic superheroes Flash (as Jay Garrick) and Hawkman (Carter Hall) first appear in "Flash Comics No. 1" published by DC

1st episode of Jay Ward's cartoon series Rocky and His Friends", featuring Rocket J. ("Rocky") Squirrel and Bullwinkle J. Moose. airs on ABC; voice talents of June Foray, Paul Frees, and William Conrad
US PresidentAbraham Lincolndelivers his Gettysburg address beginning; "Four score and seven years ago..."
Longest partial lunar eclipse since 1440, lasting 3 hours, 28 minutes and 23 seconds
LeBron Jameshits 25 points, 11 rebounds & 10 assists as LA Lakers' beat Oklahoma City Thunder, 112-107 to become first player in NBA history to record a triple-double against all 30 franchises
Kellogg's Pop Tarts pastries created
Mongol army lands at Hakata Bay in Japan during their first invasion attempt and are defeated; a typhoon destroys most of their fleet as they withdraw
Lewis and ClarkExpedition, led byMeriwether LewisandWilliam Clark, reaches the Pacific Ocean, the first European Americans to cross the West
The 12.9 km Confederation Bridge, joining Borden-Carleton, Prince Edward Island and Cape Jourimain, New-Brunswick is completed and becomes the longest bridge over ice covered waters in the world

Rams beat Kansas City Chiefs, 54-51 at Los Angeles Memorial Coliseum; 3rd-highest scoring game in NFL history; first time 2 teams score 50 points in same game;Patrick Mahomes' 6 TD passes for KC most in "Monday Night Football" history
North American F-86 Sabre sets world aircraft speed record, 1124 KPH
"The Miracle at the Meadowlands", Eagles Herman Edwards returns a fumble for a touchdown with 31 seconds left to give Philadelphia a 19-17 victory over the rival New York Giants
Jari Kurribecomes the first (and only) Finnish player to score five goals in a game
Space Shuttle STS 80 (Columbia 21), launches into space
Disney World's Fort Wilderness opens
Eileen Collins, American USAF and astronaut (STS 63, 84), born in Elmira, New York
US PresidentRonald Reaganand Soviet leaderMikhail Gorbachevmeet for the first time
E.D. Barbour of Boston is awarded the first U.S. patent for the first 'calculator', an adding machine capable of printing totals and subtotals
A meteorite lands near the village of Großliebenthal, southwest of Odessa, Ukraine
The Mayflower reaches Cape Cod and explores the coast
China launches its first Shenzhou spacecraft, Shenzhou 1, in an uncrewed flight
US beats Trinidad, 1-0 qualifing for 1990 world soccer cup finals it was US' 1st qualification since 1950
Joe Kershalla scores 71 points in a college football game
Brazil records its highest-ever temperature - 44.8C (112.6F) in Araçuaí, Minas Gerais state
200" mirror arrives at Mt Palomar Observatory, California
American inventor Frederick E. Blaisdell of Philadelphia patents the "self-sharpening" paper-sheathed pencil, commonly known as a china marker
"The Beatles Anthology" documentary series premieres in US on ABC-TV; the 6-hour program airs over three nights that week
NY receives firstMarconiwireless transmission from Italy
Savion Glover, American dancer and choreographer (Bring in 'da Noise), born in Newark, New Jersey
Gail Devers, American athlete (Olympics gold 100m 1992, 96), born in Seattle, Washington
Sushmita Sen, Indian actress and model, crowned 43rd Miss Universe in 1994, born in Hyderabad, Andhra Pradesh, India
K'ak Chan Yopaat [Smoke Snake] aged 15 becomes the 11th ruler of the Maya city of Copán, will go on to rule for 49 years
MLB Cleveland Indians name change to Guardians becomes official
American poet and abolitionist Julia Ward Howe commits lyrics of "Battle Hymn of the Republic" to paper
Vincent van Gogh's "Portrait of the Artist Without Beard" sells at auction for $71.5 million
The Weekndreleases his single "Blinding Lights" - goes on to become the longest charting song on Billboards Top 100 at 90 weeks
Christopher Columbusfirst reaches Puerto Rico, inhabited by the Taíno on his 2nd voyage to the Caribbean

ICC Men's Cricket ODI World Cup, Narendra Modi Stadium, Ahmedabad, India: Australia beats India by 6 wickets (with 42 balls remaining) for 6th ODI triumph; Player of the Match: Travis Head (AUS) 137 (120)
German tennis No. 1Steffi Grafoutlasts No. 2Anke Huber6–1, 2–6, 6–1, 4–6, 6–3 to win the WTA Tour Championship at Madison Square Garden, NYC
Home crowd favoriteBoris Beckerwins his 3rd and final ATP Tour World Championship tennis title with a 7–6, 6–0, 7–6 win over AmericanMichael Changin Frankfurt, Germany
Swiss tennis starMartina Hingiswins WTA Tour Championship at Madison Square Garden, NYC; beatsMonica Seles, 6–7, 6–4, 6–4
Bob Boone, American baseball catcher (4×MLB All-Star; World Series 1980 Philadelphia Phillies; 7×Gold Glove Award) and manager (KC Royals, Cincinnati Reds), born in San Diego, California
Jean-Francois Clervoy, French engineer and astronaut (STS 66, 84), born in Longeville, France
Ofra Haza, Yemeni-Israeli singer, born in Tel Aviv, Israel (d. 2000)
Erika Alexander, American actress (The Cosby Show, American Fiction), born in Winslow, Arizona
Terrence "T.C." Carson, American actor (Living Single), born in Chicago, Illinois
Ryukishi07 [real name unknown), Japanese mystery writer (When They Cry series), born in Chiba Prefecture, Japan
Lakshmi Bai, Indian Queen of the princely state of Jhansi in North India and leader of the Indian Mutiny (1857-58), born in Benares, Kingdom of Kashi-Benares (Varanasi) (d. 1858)
James B. Sumner, American chemist (Nobel 1946 - discovered that enzymes can be crystallized), born in Canton, Massachusetts (d. 1955)

Hendrik Christoffel Van de Hulst, Dutch astronomer (correctly predicted the existence of the 21 cm hyper-fine line of neutral atomic hydrogen in interstellar space), born in Utrecht, Netherlands (d. 2000)
Yuri Knorozov, Russian linguist and epigrapher (major role indeciphering Mayan script), born in Kharkov, Ukraine, U.S.S.R. (d. 1999)
George E. Palade, Romanian-American cell biologist (ribosomen, Nobel 1974), born in Iași, Romania (d. 2008)

Stanley Keith Runcorn, British geophysicist (pioneering studies of paleomagnetism provided early evidence in support of the theory of continental drift), born in Southport, Lancashire, England (d. 1995)
Tatyana Afanasyeva, Russian-Dutch mathematician and physicist (fields of statistical mechanics and statistical thermodynamics), born in Kyiv, Ukraine (d. 1964)
